summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View column about algorithmic fairness. The post itself contains no substantive argument, serving purely as a promotional link to her external writing. The referenced piece addresses the challenge of defining and encoding fairness into algorithmic systems. O'Neil suggests that increased reliance on algorithms necessitates grappling with fundamental questions about what fairness means.
key_points: Algorithms require explicit definitions of fairness to function equitably
Growing algorithmic dependence makes defining fairness an urgent priority
The post serves as a gateway to O'Neil's mainstream media writing on algorithmic accountability
money_quotes: If we're going to rely on algorithms, we'll have to figure it out.
key_insight: As algorithms increasingly govern our lives, we must first solve the philosophical problem of defining fairness before we can encode it into machines.
